## [12:06 - 15:07]

and the way I teach some of the clients that I work with is really, I call it the bullseye, right? When traffic comes to your website, they become aware of you. They're a lead, they're a prospect. Maybe they give you their email, maybe they don't, but the point is they're aware of your brand, right? And all you need to do is get them to become a customer. And when you get to them to become a customer, now you've got their credit card, they start an experience with you. The next step we focus on right there, get them to become a member. Because that's where we know the big, big bucks are going to be made off those people. And that's where we know those members, those people who came in, became customers, became members. The next step I call the believers. The believers are those people in your CRM when you open it up and sort by a lifetime value and your jaw hits the floor. And you go, how did this person spend $6,000 starting with a free plus shipping cookbook, right? And those believers buy everything, they do anything. Those are the people you're going to sell those high ticket items to as well. But every single one of those phases is really important. All I try to do is approach our funnel design by the way of looking at this bullseye and just trying to escalate them closer to the middle on every single step of the process. So what effect will doing membership in your business have on your business? This is a free plus shipping funnel that we've been running for a while. We didn't have membership in it originally and we had a $21.52 lifetime value, not including email revenue. So it's really basically day one cart value beforehand, which is surprisingly difficult to make work on paid media because you have to pay for a CPA, you have to pay for the product and the shipping and stuff like that. So we were still taking some time to break even. After we implemented membership in the upsells, the lifetime value was $41.01. Again, that was without email revenue. So almost 100% increase by just focusing on the product being a loss leader and the main stay of what we're trying to get them to do is to join our membership program. All right, so a $39.00 VSL quiz funnel, same thing happened, right? Before we had membership in there, we had a $73.84 day one cart value. Isn't bad, but at some point the cost to acquire traffic to that got more than I could afford. It got to the $75.80, $85.00, $100.10 range. We implemented membership and LTV, again, nearly doubled, almost exactly doubled. So $146.41, again, not including email revenue off of that as well. So I cannot tell you guys how much taking your product or your brand and trying to figure out how to create a subscription component to this is going to make a huge difference on your company. I echo the sentiments that Jeff and Mark said. I think last year, 55% somewhere around there, of our revenue came from that back end, which was membership, which was email, which was a ton of different stuff like that. That's not necessarily the case, but it's certainly extremely helpful to know that before you get started. So, but I think that's great, but I think the biggest thing that going with a subscription model for the majority of your transactions will do for you is if you focus on product development, reducing churn will make the biggest impact on your bottom line and it will make customers happier. into our membership program. We really wanted to compete, like I said, again with humongous companies like Weight Watchers and Peloton. We started going, okay, let's get on an acquisition spree. That's great. Don't get me wrong. But at some point you look at the back end of that business and you go, how many people am I going to have to acquire a day to get to that million member goal? And so we all took a moment to breathe and go, okay, we are not ever going to get to our goal using this strategy. So we've been focusing immensely on product development, particularly for our membership product for the last nine months and very, very heavily for the last six or so months. The reason why I say that is because when I did a look at 2019, I found out that every month I would have added to my churn rate was worth $1.7 million. So that's what we've been focusing on in big time. I think it's really a next step. But the absolute biggest thing that this will do for you guys, it's going to make your company worth money to someone who wants to buy it.
